channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Basketball Classics now available for PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Xbox One, and Switch
item.content:encoded:
8-bit basketball game Basketball Classics is now available for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, and Switch for $9.99 with a 10 percent-off launch discount, publisher Acclaim and developer Namco Gamo announced.

Basketball Classics first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on March 24, 2018, followed by a full release on December 18, 2019. The PC version is also now available via Microsoft Store.

“This has been a childhood dream of ours,” said Namo Gamo co-founder David Pilkington in a press release. “Now, almost a decade in the making, Basketball Classics will be available to new users on consoles worldwide. Thanks to Acclaim for making this dream a reality. We can’t wait to follow this up with Super Basketball Classics.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Acclaim:

Five-on-five professional basketball born from the golden era of sports games. This spiritual successor to the 8-bit ballers bridges the gap between retro and today’s simulation games.

  • Side-scrolling, sweaty arcade action.
  • Simplified three-button gameplay.
  • Decades of rosters with over 175 teams.
  • Over 1,000 players with individualized attributes.
  • Seamless play calling.
  • Sensational cut scenes.
  • One-player Story Mode unlocks secret teams.
  • Scream-inducing local multiplayer.
  • Retro vibes galore.
  • No virtual currency. No pay-to-win. No micro transactions. Ever.

Side-scrolling, arcade action is paired with on-the-fly strategy. Three-button gameplay is easy to pick up, but it is the dynamic play-calling that elevates this title beyond just long bombs and acrobatics.

Attribute-rich players make up a vast set of rosters. Dig through decades of teams to find your favorite All-Stars and even unlock the “Legends” in an immersive story mode.

This game is a passion project for us indie developers here at Namo Gamo. We hope you can feel the love put into every pixel, the endless adjustments to stat-driven gameplay, and the genuinely retro sound and music.

Watch a new trailer below.

Consoles Launch Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/basketball-classics-now-available-for-ps5-xbox-series-ps4-xbox-one-and-switch
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Two Point Museum x Angry Birds collaboration update now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

New spring seasonal content and quality-of-life updates also available.

Publisher SEGA and developer Two Point Studios have released the version 8.0 update for Two Point Museum, which brings Rovio’s Angry Birds to the museum’s Digiverse, the companies announced. The update is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC, and is coming soon to Switch 2.

Here is an overview of the update, via SEGA:

The Digiverse is an ever-expanding expedition map in Two Point Museum that allows players to explore new Points of Interest, exhibits and decorations from different universes.

The latest rift will ruffle some feathers as Angry Birds slingshots into Two Point County. Players will be able to employ Red and the gang to help excite bird-loving guests and fend off mischievous Bad Piggies from trotting all over the exhibits. The rift will also open up new areas such as Piggy Island, where explorers can discover artefacts from the Angry Birds universe and set up the first ever bird wildlife exhibit. Full details can be found in the blog post on Steam and G2G.

Alongside the Angry Birds Digiverse Rift, curators will be able to give their museums a spring clean with a seasonal update that includes brand new items to freshen up the halls. There will be adventures to embark on with new Points of Interest in the Easter Isles, where players can discover some fluffy critters that have come out of hibernation, helping encourage a new visitor to attend their exhibition—the Egg Hunters.

Available now, the free update will also be jam-packed with new content and quality-of-life changes. Well-seasoned explorers will be able to get new Curator-Class rewards for higher levels of museum management, outdoor items such as Picnic Tables will arrive in time for warmer weather and Briny Bay will now be full of marine life for water exhibits. All of this and more will help managers keep their museum busy and bright for spring.

  • Angry Birds Update
    • Explore Piggy Island, visiting three new Points of Interest: Cobalt Plateaus, Bamboo Forest and Pig City.
    • Dress your guests with Angry Birds merch with new Gift Shop items.
    • Collect nine exhibits, including an interactive display and new roaming Wildlife exhibits.
    • Decorate with new items, wallpaper and flooring to create your very own Angryverse Museum.
  • Spring Seasonal Update
    • Embark to two new Points of Interest that make up the Easter Isles, hidden away in Scorched Earth.
    • Explore three new exhibits, including a cute and fluffy Habitat critter and Botany discoveries.
    • Make your museum blossom with new décor items and customization options for your walls and floor.
    • Lure in customers with new Chocolate Cake Kiosks and baskets of chocolate eggs.

Two Point Museum is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam.

Watch a new set of trailers below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Angry Birds Collaboration Trailer

Spring Seasonal Update Trailer

Version 8.0 Update Trailer

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯
